steps.arg: If you want to see more examples , see  List Of RSCG
My name is Andrei Ignat and I am deeply fond of Roslyn Source Code Generator.

Today I will present Aigamo.MatchGenerator . Generates exhaustive Match() extension methods for enums and abstract record hierarchies (tagged unions), ensuring compile-time safety — all cases must be handled.How to use 1. Add [GenerateMatch] attribute to an enum or abstract record: [GenerateMatch] public enum CarTypes { None, Dacia, Tesla, BMW, Mercedes } [GenerateMatch] abstract record MaritalStatus; sealed record Single : MaritalStatus; sealed record Married : MaritalStatus; 2. Use the generated .Match() method — all cases required (compile error if any missing): var msg = car.Match(     onBMW: () => "this is bmw",     onDacia: () => "this is dacia",     onMercedes: () => "this is mercedes",     onNone: () => "this is none",     onTesla: () => "this is tesla" );You can use for  Transforming enums/records into exhaustive pattern matching (discriminated unions style). If a case is missing in Match(), it's a compile-time error .
You can download the code from here
So , let's start the project with Visual Studio Code
To use it ,you will put the Nuget Aigamo.MatchGenerator into the csproj
And now I will show you an example of using Aigamo.MatchGenerator
now execute the tour in VSCode
And I will execute the project
